title: Daily Word
description: A 5-minute daily devotional exploring God's Word, one verse at a time.

1.title: The God Who Sees
1.description: Exploring Genesis 16 and Hagar's encounter with the Living God who sees her in her suffering and calls her by name.
keyPoints: God sees us in our deepest struggles
Hagar named God — 'El Roi', the God who sees
No wilderness is hidden from God's gaze

transcript.text: Welcome to the Daily Word from The Bible Scrollers.
Today we are looking at Genesis chapter 16, verse 13.
And she called the name of the LORD that spake unto her, Thou God seest me.
Hagar was a woman in crisis. Cast out, alone in the wilderness, pregnant and afraid.
But in that moment of absolute desperation, God met her. Not in a temple, not on a mountain — in a desert.
She gave God a name no one else in Scripture did: El Roi — the God who sees me.
Think about that. The creator of the universe, and Hagar's testimony is: He sees ME.
Whatever wilderness you find yourself in today, know this: you are not invisible to God.
He sees your struggle, He knows your name, and He is present even when you cannot see Him.
Let that truth settle into your heart today. You serve the God who sees. Amen.

2.title: Be Still and Know
2.description: Finding peace and trust in God's sovereignty through the powerful words of Psalm 46.
keyPoints: God is our refuge and strength
Stillness is an act of trust, not passivity
God's sovereignty extends over all chaos

transcript.text: Welcome to the Daily Word from The Bible Scrollers.
Today our passage comes from Psalm 46, verse 10.
Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
In a world that never stops moving, God's command is strikingly countercultural: be still.
This is not a suggestion to be passive. It is an invitation to trust.
The Hebrew word here — raphah — means to let go, to cease striving, to surrender control.
When the nations rage, when the earth shakes, when everything around us feels unstable — God says: I am still God.
Your stillness before Him is not weakness. It is the deepest expression of faith.
Today, wherever the noise is loudest, take a moment. Be still. And know.
He is God. He has always been God. And He will be exalted. Rest in that. Amen.
